summary: Ukraine announced an additional 10.8 billion UAH allocation for weapons procurement and domestic defence industry expansion. The Ukrainian General Staff reported recapturing roughly 46 km² around several villages, including near Novoselivka. Russian forces continued launching Shahed drones and missiles toward multiple Ukrainian regions, including Vinnytsia and Cherkasy.
key_points: Ukraine earmarked 10.8 bn UAH for new weapons, equipment repair, and OPOK development, funded by income tax and gambling licences (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).
Ukrainian forces liberated territory near Novoselivka and cleared enemy from villages such as Voronny, Sichne, Piddubne, and others, totaling at least 46 km² (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Large numbers of Shahed‑type UAVs were launched from Kursk, indicating a possible mass attack (Source: @eRadarrua/94160).
Two rockets struck Vinnytsia and one rocket hit northern Cherkasy on a western trajectory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi and Izmail districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Multiple UAV groups were reported near the Sumy‑Kharkiv border moving toward Konotop and other points (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
Russian President Putin claimed Russia seeks a peaceful solution to the Ukraine conflict (Source: @tass_agency/378139).
Ukraine’s parliament approved a record defence budget increase to 1.56 trn UAH and a €90 bn EU loan agreement (Source: @UkraineNow/81523).

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine allocated 10.8 bn UAH for weapons and defence industry growth, while its forces reclaimed roughly 46 km² around several villages. Russian forces intensified aerial attacks, launching Shahed drones from Kursk and rockets into Vinnytsia and Cherkasy. Both sides continue high‑tempo operations across multiple fronts.

points: The Ukrainian government directed an extra 10.8 bn UAH to defence procurement, equipment repair, and domestic arms industry scaling, financed by personal income tax and gambling licence revenues (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).

The General Staff confirmed Ukrainian troops cleared enemy from areas near Novoselivka and conducted sweeps around Voronny, Sichne, Piddubne, Tovsty, Novokhatke, and Zelenyi Hai, resulting in a loss of at least 46 km² for the adversary (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Parliament approved a historic defence budget increase to 1.56 trn UAH and ratified a €90 bn EU loan, bolstering long‑term military financing (Source: @UkraineNow/81523).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV interceptions along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, targeting routes toward Konotop and other strategic points (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
A Ukrainian drone strike reportedly disabled a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence node in the Krasnodar region, halting its missile and UAV guidance capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
Russian forces launched Shahed‑type UAVs from Kursk in what appears to be a mass strike, increasing pressure on Ukrainian air defences (Source: @eRadarrua/94160).
Two rockets impacted Vinnytsia and one struck northern Cherkasy, travelling on a western course, indicating continued long‑range missile use (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi and Izmail districts, suggesting maritime targeting (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Russian President Vladimir Putin publicly stated Russia seeks a peaceful resolution to the Ukraine conflict (Source: @tass_agency/378139).
Russian media reported the liberation of ten settlements in the “Special Military Operation” zone over a week, reflecting ongoing offensive actions (Source: @tass_agency/378103).
Ukrainian forces cleared enemy from villages near Voronny, Sichne, Piddubne, Tovsty, Novokhatke, and Zelenyi Hai, reclaiming at least 46 km² (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Shahed UAV groups were reported moving along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border toward Konotop, indicating heightened aerial activity in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
Multiple UAV launches were noted from the Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/63218).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the south‑east direction, posing a threat to front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV activity near Pavlohrad and Orzhytsia, suggesting eastward pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/94188).
Two rockets struck Vinnytsia, and one rocket hit northern Cherkasy, both on western trajectories, showing Russian strike capability toward central‑north Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near the Sumy‑Kharkiv border moving westward toward Kotelva, indicating surveillance of northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/63221).
A missile was reported over Chernihiv, highlighting continued northern missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67260).
Shahed drone activity was noted over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa and Izmail, affecting northern coastal zones (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV groups near the Sumy‑Kharkiv border moving toward Konotop from the south‑east, affecting northern operational depth (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi and Izmail districts, indicating southern maritime pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Ukrainian UAV groups were reported in the Kherson region moving toward Dnipropetrovsk, suggesting southern‑to‑central strike routes (Source: @eRadarrua/94178).
Russian missile launches targeted Vinnytsia and Cherkasy, regions south of the central axis, reflecting broader southern strike patterns (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
A Russian attack in Zaporizhzhia damaged four houses and injured civilians, showing ground‑level impact in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81525).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity near Orikhiv and Gulaypol in Zaporizhzhia, indicating ongoing southern air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94155).
Large numbers of Shahed UAVs launched from Kursk suggest a coordinated aerial offensive by Russia (Source: @eRadarrua/94160).
Multiple rocket impacts in Vinnytsia and Cherkasy demonstrate continued Russian missile use against Ukrainian interior targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border moving toward Konotop and Kotelva, indicating active Ukrainian air‑reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/63219).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the south‑east direction, raising the risk of air strikes on near‑front zones (Source: @kpszsu/63223).
Ukrainian drone strike disabled a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence node in Krasnodar, reducing Russian targeting capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).

key_developments: Ukraine allocated an additional 10.8 bn UAH for weapons procurement, equipment repair, and domestic defence industry scaling, funded by income tax and gambling licence revenues (Source: @DeepStateUA/23543).
Ukrainian forces reclaimed roughly 46 km² around Novoselivka and cleared enemy from villages including Voronny, Sichne, and Piddubne (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Shahed‑type UAVs were launched in large numbers from Kursk, indicating a possible mass aerial attack (Source: @eRadarrua/94160).
Two rockets struck Vinnytsia and one hit northern Cherkasy on western courses, showing continued Russian long‑range missile activity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Eight Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi and Izmail districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67255).
Ukrainian parliament approved a record defence budget increase to 1.56 trn UAH and a €90 bn EU loan, strengthening long‑term military financing (Source: @UkraineNow/81523).
A Ukrainian drone strike disabled a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence node in the Krasnodar region, disrupting enemy missile and UAV guidance (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial activity remains intense, with mass Shahed UAV launches from Kursk and missile strikes on central Ukrainian cities, posing a sustained threat to air defence and critical infrastructure. Ukrainian counter‑offensives have reclaimed territory in the east, but continued UAV incursions along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border suggest persistent pressure on front‑line logistics. The disabling of a Russian FSB electronic node indicates Ukraine's growing capability to degrade Russian targeting networks.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are increasing financial support, exemplified by Ukraine's record defence budget and a €90 bn EU loan, while Sweden pledged Gripen fighters, enhancing air‑defence capacity. Russian diplomatic statements claim a desire for peace, yet operational actions contradict this narrative, maintaining high tension across all fronts. Regional actors remain vigilant as both sides sustain high‑tempo operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed UAV launch sites, especially Kursk, is critical for early warning. Continued observation of rocket trajectories toward Vinnytsia and Cherkasy will aid in predicting future strike patterns. Ukrainian recapture of eastern villages should be tracked for potential exploitation of weakened Russian positions. The impact of the newly funded defence budget on procurement timelines warrants close analysis.
